results.abstract: <jats:p>This systematic literature review examines the impact of generative artificial intelligence (AI) applications on student learning outcomes in middle school science education. Twelve studies that met the inclusion and exclusion criteria were included in the study. The studies were accessed from the WOS, SCOPUS, ERIC, and TrDizin indexes and databases. The PRISMA protocol was used in the selection of studies. The analysis reveals that generative AI tools significantly contribute to academi
results.tldr: The analysis reveals that generative AI tools significantly contribute to academic achievement, conceptual understanding, scientific and digital literacy, personalized learning opportunities, and students' motivational and emotional outcomes.

results.title: The Impact of AI-Powered Personalized Learning on ESL Student Outcomes
results.venue: EuroGlobal Journal of Linguistics and Language Education
results.abstract: <jats:p>This study investigates the impact of AI-powered personalized learning on ESL student outcomes through a mixed-methods approach involving 120 university-level learners. Results demonstrate statistically significant improvements in language proficiency (p < .001), with notable gains in vocabulary retention (40%), grammar accuracy (25%), and pronunciation (35%) compared to traditional methods (Jarrah et al., 2023; Ganesh dandu & G. Mohanacharyulu, 2023). Qualitative data reveal enha
results.tldr: Qualitative data reveal enhanced student engagement through gamified AI tools and increased self-directed learning behaviors and recommendations for ethical AI integration, hybrid pedagogical models, and targeted teacher training to optimize ESL outcomes globally.

results.title: Impact of Technology on Student Learning Outcomes: Examining Digital Tools, Online Platforms, and AI in Modern Education
results.abstract: <jats:p>This paper investigates the influence of technology on student learning outcomes, focusing on the role of digital tools, online platforms, and artificial intelligence (AI) in shaping contemporary education. With the rapid advancement of technology, educational institutions are increasingly integrating these tools into their teaching practices. This study reviews existing literature and analyzes empirical research to assess how technology enhances student engagement, motivation, and acade
results.tldr: Findings indicate that technology, when effectively integrated, can lead to improved learning outcomes; however, successful adoption requires thoughtful instructional design and adequate support for both students and educators.

results.abstract: <jats:p>The rapid advancement of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI) has significantly transformed assessment and feedback mechanisms in higher education. Traditional assessment practices often suffer from delayed feedback, limited personalization, and high faculty workload, which may negatively affect student engagement and learning outcomes. results.title: The Impact of Technology Integration on Student Engagement and Learning Outcomes in Secondary Education
results.abstract: <jats:p>This study examines the influence of technology integration on student engagement and learning outcomes within secondary education settings. Employing a mixed-methods research design, data were collected through surveys, classroom observations, and academic performance records from a sample of 300 students and 20 teachers across five secondary schools in Ibadan, Nigeria. The Impact of AI-Powered Adaptive Learning on Student Outcomes
results.abstract: <jats:p>This paper examines the influence of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered adaptive learning systems on various student outcomes, including academic performance, engagement, motivation, and retention. Drawing on a systematic review of contemporary literature, the study synthesizes findings that overwhelmingly point to the positive effect of personalization on learning efficiency and achievement. results.title: AI-Supported Language Learning for Neurodiverse Students in Low-Resource Contexts: Student and Teacher Perspectives and Its Impact on Learning Outcomes

results.title: Mitigating Cognitive Biases in Education: The Role of AI-Generated Quizzes in Enhancing Student Learning Outcomes
results.abstract: <jats:p>The study of learning biases school students face is gaining more attention in education psychology. The present research explores how different cognitive biases influence how students perceive, process, and retain information. Various learning biases, such as confirmation bias, Dunning-Kruger effect, Halo effect, sunk cost fallacy, bandwagon effect, self-serving bias, fixed mindset, recency effect, anchoring bias, and others, are responsible for shaping students' educational experiences

results.abstract: <jats:p>During a college undergraduate's scholastic experience, it should be recognized that learning occurs in many ways, in different places and times, and through a variety of interactions. Deep and enhanced learning transpires when individuals are provided with a complex set of activities to grasp emerging concepts. results.abstract: <jats:p>The current study depicts that the avatar representation in the metaverse has a significant amount of influence on the student engagement and learning outcomes in the metaverse educational contexts.
